perturbation.original_text: 3. "Product" means: The products and materials the Company develops, creates or provides in connection with its educational training, products and materials relating to the Property Training Course.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. "Product" means: The services the Company develops, creates or provides in connection with its educational training, products and materials relating to the Property Training Course.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'products and materials' to 'services' creates a contradiction. Throughout the contract, 'Product' is used in the context of physical items. Now 'Product' also means 'services', this creates potential ambiguity. For example, section 4 refers to 'sales of the Property Training Course and all Products', it now becomes unclear if this includes services related to the course, affecting compensation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. COMPENSATION In consideration for the licenses granted hereunder, Company agrees to pay to CSA as follows: a. A [***]in the amount of [***]of Company's revenues from sales of the Property Training Course and all Products after deductions for VAT, returns, refunds b. Company shall be responsible for the tracking of sales of the Property Training Course and all Products containing the Property and providing to CSA: i. A monthly list of the sales of Property Training Course and Product (with supporting sales prices and reconciled reports). ii. Payment to CSA no later than thirty (30) days after receipt of invoice from CSA. CSA shall submit invoice to the Company no later than thirty (30) days after Company provides CSA with the list of the sales of Product (containing the Property with such supporting information that may be required or requested to be disclosed). c. CSA shall have the right to request an independent audit of the sales of the Product containing the Property which the Company agrees to assist within a reasonable period of time of such request.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'products and materials' to 'services' in the definition of 'Product' creates a direct contradiction with Section 4, which outlines compensation based on 'sales of the Property Training Course and all Products'. The original definition implied physical items, allowing for clear sales tracking. By including 'services' in the definition, it introduces ambiguity as to whether the compensation scheme also includes services related to the course. The contradicted text references 'sales of Product', which, after the change, now also includes 'services'. This creates confusion and ambiguity about whether service revenues are included in the sales calculation for compensation and the independent audit in section 4, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. REVIEW OF MATERIALS a. Talent shall have the right of approval in respect of all Product being proposed as any part of the Property appearing or integrated into it and all proposed use of the Property (including to whom the Property is distributed for sale if other than the general public) prior to public release and distribution.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. REVIEW OF MATERIALS a. Talent shall have the right of consultation in respect of all Product being proposed as any part of the Property appearing or integrated into it and all proposed use of the Property (including to whom the Property is distributed for sale if other than the general public) prior to public release and distribution.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'approval' to 'consultation' weakens Talent's control over the use of his image. 'Approval' implies a veto power, while 'consultation' suggests that the company can proceed even if Talent disagrees. This change causes conflict in interpreting the extent of Talent's control over the use of his 'Property', specifically his image.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. REVIEW OF MATERIALS a. Talent shall have the right of consultation in respect of all Product being proposed as any part of the Property appearing or integrated into it and all proposed use of the Property (including to whom the Property is distributed for sale if other than the general public) prior to public release and distribution. b. All Materials or any part thereof shall be sent by the Company to CSA for approval by Talent. c. Talent shall have seven (7) days following receipt of such Materials or any part thereof displaying the intended use of the Property to review and for CSA to provide to Company written approval for such use. d. In the event that Talent objects to the Products or any part thereof and/or any proposed use of the Property, CSA on behalf of Talent shall submit to Company, within seven (7) days following Company's official submission of materials, a written request for revisions. Talent shall not make any request for unreasonable revisions and shall not withhold consent for any proposed use of the Property unreasonably. e. In the event that Talent does not provide either written approval of materials or a written request for revisions of such materials containing a proposed use of the Property within ten (10) days following Company's submission of such materials to Talent for review, such non-response shall automatically be deemed to be an acceptance and approval of the proposed use of the Property.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted Talent 'approval' rights, which implies a veto power. The changed text weakens this to 'consultation', meaning the company can potentially proceed even if Talent disagrees. The contradicted text outlines a detailed process for approval, revisions, and deemed acceptance based on non-response. By weakening Talent's initial right to 'consultation' instead of 'approval', the process for revision and approval becomes contradictory, as Talent no longer possesses the initial veto power the process implies. The change directly impacts Talent's control and creates a clear conflict regarding the intended approval mechanism.

perturbation.original_text: 8. TERMINATION a. Except as provided in this Section 8, this Agreement shall terminate immediately upon the earlier of (i) the Term of this Agreement or (ii) dissolving of Company and/or Company's complete cessation of doing all business.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. TERMINATION a. Except as provided in this Section 8, this Agreement shall terminate immediately upon the earlier of (i) the Term of this Agreement or (ii) dissolving of Company and/or Company's substantial cessation of doing business.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'complete cessation' to 'substantial cessation' creates ambiguity regarding the threshold for termination. 'Complete cessation' is a clear, objective standard, whereas 'substantial cessation' is subjective and open to interpretation. This change leads to a contradiction when interpreting termination rights, as it's unclear what level of business activity triggers the termination clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. TERMINATION a. Except as provided in this Section 8, this Agreement shall terminate immediately upon the earlier of (i) the Term of this Agreement or (ii) dissolving of Company and/or Company's substantial cessation of doing business. b. Either party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement immediately in the event that Talent or the Company does any of the following: i. Engages in illegal, immoral, or criminal conduct resulting in a felony conviction; ii. Misrepresents or conceals anything in their background that could be detrimental to the value of the endorsement being made; iii. Engages in conduct contrary to the best interests of the other party; iv. Engages in conduct that reasonably offends the sensitivities of a significant portion of the population; or v. Engages in conduct that could bring the other party into public disrepute. c. Either party may terminate this provision at any time during the Term, in the event either party is guilty of a material breach of this Agreement, having been given notice of such breach and the breach not being rectified within a reasonable period of time.
perturbation.justification: While the change from 'complete cessation' to 'substantial cessation' introduces ambiguity, it doesn't create a *direct* contradiction with the rest of the clause or the document. Both the changed and original text can coexist without rendering the termination clause unenforceable or inherently inconsistent. A court might need to interpret 'substantial,' but the clause itself is not internally contradictory. It doesn't change any eligibility, remove key terms, or create incompatible obligations.
